channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Southern Quebec Schools Close Early Due to Forecasted Freezing Rain
item.description: School boards across southern Quebec have announced pre-emptive closures on Wednesday due to a major freezing rain storm forecasted by Environment Canada. The Centre de services scolaire de Montréal (...

item.title: Ombudsman moves to freeze properties of Revilla and Discaya amid graft cases
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman announced it will request writs of preliminary attachment to place the properties of former Senator Ramon “Bong” Revilla Jr. and contractor Sarah Discaya under the custody ...

item.title: Canadian First World War soldiers’ remains returned by Philadelphia museum for proper burial
item.description: A Philadelphia medical history museum has returned partial remains of Canadian soldiers from the First World War, ensuring they are properly interred in the graves of the men from whom they were taken...

item.title: Public Opinion Opposes Trump’s Military Action in Iran Amid Weak National Narrative
item.description: President Donald Trump's war against Iran stands out historically for its lack of public support. Unlike previous U.S.-led regime change conflicts, which often gained broad backing through compelling ...

item.title: How Alcohol Marketing Uses Gendered Imagery to Target Women in Australia
item.description: Recent research from South Australia has examined how alcohol packaging and labelling are deliberately designed to appeal to women through gendered cues. Over two years, researchers visited ten bottle...

item.title: HMS Dragon Deployed to Cyprus Amid Regional Security Concerns Following Iranian Drone Strike
item.description: HMS Dragon, a Type 45 Royal Navy destroyer, has departed Portsmouth to support the defence of Cyprus following recent Iranian attacks on RAF Akrotiri. The deployment was ordered over a week ago, but d...

item.title: Fan interrupts German second-division football match by unplugging VAR equipment
item.description: During a Bundesliga 2 match between Preussen Munster and Hertha Berlin, a masked fan from the home supporters' section illegally entered the pitch and unplugged the VAR (Video Assistant Referee) monit...

item.title: Iranian Official Responds to Trump Threats Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: A senior Iranian security official, Ali Larijani, warned U.S. President Donald Trump to be careful after Trump threatened to hit Iran harder if Tehran tried to block oil shipments through the Strait o...

item.title: Government medical colleges urged to ensure food services amid LPG shortage
item.description: The Directorate of Medical Education and Research (DME) has issued instructions to all deans of government medical college hospitals to organise meetings with their administrative teams. This initiati...

item.title: Cabinet approves amendments to broaden definition of transgender persons under law
item.description: New Delhi: The Union Cabinet on Tuesday approved a proposal to amend the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019. The objective is to make the law more inclusive and ensure that all genui...

item.title: Five Cosmetic Surgeries with Serious Health Risks
item.description: Nowadays, almost everybody dey chase fine body shape – small waist, bigger lips, and fuller buttocks. But make person sabi say cosmetic surgery no be play. Among all, Brazilian Butt Lift (BBL) dey con...

item.title: Bongi Mlotshwa reflects on her career and views on beauty ahead of Safta nomination
item.description: Bongi Mlotshwa, one of South Africa’s most renowned make-up artists, is once again in the spotlight as she vies for another SA Film and TV Award (Safta) for her work in the TV comedy How to Ruin Love....

item.title: Choice ranks top and budget-friendly toilet paper options in Australia
item.description: A recent evaluation by consumer review group Choice has put more than 40 of Australia's most popular toilet paper products under the microscope, testing for softness, disintegration time, tearability,...

item.title: President Ruto Urges KHRC to Draft Bill Safeguarding Protesters' Rights
item.description: President William Ruto has called on the Kenya Human Rights Commission (KHRC) to come up with a law that will protect the rights of Kenyans when they are protesting. Speaking at a joint parliamentary ...

item.title: Farage's Derbyshire petrol station stunt highlights inconsistent energy and foreign policy
item.description: Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, staged a highly publicised fuel price stunt at a petrol station in Derbyshire alongside Robert Jenrick, aiming to criticise government energy policy. The event invol...

item.title: White House Permits India to Import Russian Oil Amid Global Supply Concerns
item.description: The White House has clarified its decision to allow India to resume importing Russian oil, citing that India has been a responsible partner in adhering to previous sanctions. Press Secretary Karoline ...

item.title: City of Regina advises residents to reduce recycling contamination
item.description: The City of Regina is alerting residents to increasing contamination in recycling bins, which has risen well above acceptable levels. Contamination occurs when items that should not be recycled, such ...

item.title: Tinubu greenlights fiscal incentives for $20bn Bonga deepwater oil project
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don approve special fiscal package wey go help push Final Investment Decision (FID) for the Bonga Southwest Aparo deepwater project. NNPC Ltd talk say this move na part of Nigeri...

item.title: Bournvita supports Nigerian Muslim families with nourishment and community outreach this Ramadan
item.description: For the holy month of Ramadan, Bournvita, Nigeria’s popular cocoa drink brand, don bring out one large community outreach program wey go touch more than 150,000 Nigerians. The 2026 campaign, wey dem c...

item.title: Akwa Ibom Government Clears N39.83 Billion Debt and Reduces Gratuity Backlog to N80 Billion
item.description: The Akwa Ibom State Government don announce say dem don clear all inherited bank debts wey reach N39.83 billion, including principal and interest. Na Mr. Emem Bob, the Commissioner for Finance, talk t...
